title: Innodata Q1 Revenue Jumps 54%: More Upside Ahead for the Stock?
text: Innodata delivered impressive first-quarter 2026 results, with revenues climbing to $90.1 million, representing a 54% year-over-year increase. The company exceeded analyst expectations, beating the Zacks consensus estimate by approximately 18%. The strong performance was fueled by heightened demand from major AI developers and hyperscalers. Revenue from other Big Tech customers jumped 453% year over year, demonstrating improved customer diversification. The company provides AI data engineering, model evaluation, and trust-and-safety services across the development lifecycle. Management announced a significant new engagement that could generate roughly $51 million in 2026 revenue, potentially making it Innodata's second-largest customer this year. Profitability metrics improved substantially, with adjusted gross margins expanding from 43% to 47%. Management raised full-year 2026 revenue guidance to approximately 40% growth or higher, up from the prior 35% expectation. INOD stock has gained 117.7% over the past month but trades at a forward P/E ratio of 69.64, significantly above the industry average of 26.23. The stock currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) rating.
